items.title: Lufthansa cabin crew begin strike
items.description: Hundreds of flights have been canceled as cabin crew members walk out. This is Lufthansa's third major strike this year.

items.title: EU slams Hormuz toll idea, urges unrestricted freedom of navigation
items.description: International law provides for the freedom of navigation, which means... basically no payment or toll whatsoever, says European Commission spokesman Anouar El Anouni

items.title: From the Nile to the Euphrates: Inside Israel's expansionist ambitions
items.description: Israel has never officially defined its borders, but Israeli settlers and ministers are flirting with the biblical idea of extending them far beyond the current state. What's behind the concept of "Greater Israel"?

items.title: Macron set for first meeting with Pope Leo amid calls for peace in Middle East
items.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is set to have his first face-to-face meeting with Pope Leo XIV in the Vatican Friday as the leader of the Roman Catholic church has criticised his fellow American, US President Donald Trump, for "unacceptable belligerence" during the Iran war. Macron has called for a negotiated end to the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
items.description: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

items.title: TSMC's first-quarter revenue surges as AI interest propels sales beyond market forecasts
Tanzania president orders smaller official convoys to cut fuel
items.description: Samia Suluhu Hassan is known for her extensive motorcades. Her move is part of a series of steps announced by several African governments in recent days to cope with fuel shortages and rising prices.

items.title: Polisario Front celebrates 50 years of Sahrawi Arab Democratic Republic
items.description: Western Sahara's independence movement held a military parade in a refugee camp near Tindouf in Algeria on Wednesday.

items.title: North Korea, China agree to deepen cooperation in talks between Foreign Ministers
items.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi flew to Pyongyang in his first visit to North Korea in seven years

Melania Trump says she never had a relationship with Epstein

items.title: Front-runner Wadagni eyes Presidency in Benin vote
items.description: In Benin, Finance Minister Romuald Wadagni is the clear favorite in Sunday’s presidential election, backed by outgoing president Patrice Talon, who is stepping down after two terms.

items.title: Iranians pay tribute to slain supreme leader weeks after killing
items.description: Thousands of Iranians rallied Thursday in a tribute to former supreme leader Ali Khamenei, killed on the first day of the war with the United States and Israel, expressing criticism of talks expected in Pakistan this week

items.title: Trump says U.S. military to stay deployed near Iran until 'real agreement' reached
items.description: In the meantime our great Military is Loading Up and Resting, looking forward, actually, to its next Conquest. AMERICA IS BACK, says U.S. President Donald Trump in a post on Truth Social

items.title: Iran's Supreme Leader says does not seek war in written message
items.description: "We did not seek war and we do not want it," Iran’s Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei said in the message read out on state TV

items.title: Can Africa’s informal economy become the continent’s next investment frontier? {Business Africa}
items.description: Formalising Africa’s sizable informal sector: how an investment company is turning unreported consumer power into verifiable, high-growth portfolios. While Kenya’s housing faces crisis, a massive deficit of two million units persists despite the real estate sector’s 5.7% growth.

items.title: Nigeria general killed as troops repel Borno base attack
items.description: Jihadists in northeast Nigeria killed several troops including a brigadier general in an assault on a military base, local officials said on Thursday, the second killing of a high-ranking officer in five months.

items.title: Exiled cartoonists give voice to Iran's silenced millions
items.description: Artists have used satire throughout Iran's turbulent history to circumvent the threat of persecution

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith display at War Memorial altered following arrest
items.description: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

items.title: Afrika Bambaataa, rap icon accused of sexual abuse, dead at 67
items.description: The Universal Zulu Nation founder was a beloved hip hop icon before sexual abuse and trafficking allegations surfaced in the 2010s.

items.title: Stefano Gabbana has resigned as Dolce & Gabbana chair, filing shows
items.description: It was also reported that the designer was also ‌considering options for his roughly ‌40% ⁠stake in the company ahead of ⁠negotiations with its bank lenders

items.title: Zambia: Is the US trading HIV treatment for resources?
items.description: The US is reportedly leveraging health aid to gain access to Zambia's critical minerals. Millions depend on US funding for HIV, tuberculosis and malaria treatment. Yet Zambia is hesitant to agree.

items.title: Ceasefire holds, but Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill
items.description: When will the Strait of Hormuz be open for business again? A fragile ceasefire may be in place, but for now maritime traffic through the waterway remains at a near standstill. There is growing evidence that Iran’s Revolutionary Guard has laid mines in the Strait, a move likely to deter shipping companies from making the journey. FRANCE 24 speaks to Adi Imsirovic, lecturer on the energy systems at the University of Oxford.

items.title: Pope Leo heads to Africa for first major foreign tour
items.description: Pope Leo will address a myriad of issues as he covers more than 18,000 kilometres across the African continent

items.title: Netanyahu greenlights Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington amid ceasefire tensions
items.description: Israel and Lebanon are set to hold talks in Washington next week as tensions escalate over continued fighting in Lebanon and concerns grow that the fragile US-Iran ceasefire could collapse. The meeting comes amid heavy strikes, rising casualties and renewed disputes over the scope of the truce and regional security arrangements.

items.title: South Korea Justice Minister reassures investors on corporate governance reform
Artemis II astronauts hurtle home from Moon toward Pacific splashdown
items.description: The Artemis II team headed for a Friday re-entry to Earth after a historic lunar mission, the first crewed mission to the Moon in over 50 years. The most crucial moments of the 10-day mission awaits them as they prepare for a splashdown in the Pacific Ocean off the coast of California.

items.title: Hospitals in Beirut struggle to deal with casualties after Israeli attacks
items.description: Doctors warn of worsening crises as vital supplies run low and Israeli attacks devastate Beirut and surrounding areas.

items.title: Low turnout as Djibouti votes in election set to extend Guelleh’s long rule
items.description: Low turnout marked the start of voting in Djibouti’s presidential election, with only a trickle of people showing up at polling stations in the early hours in the capital.

items.title: Melania Trump denies ties to Jeffrey Epstein and urges hearing for survivors
items.description: She called rumours that Epstein introduced her to Donald Trump "mean-spirited attempts to defame my reputation".

items.description: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

items.description: The U.S and Iran are due to meet in Islamabad, the capital of Pakistan, for talks this weekend

items.description: The Bronx born rapper's global hit Planet Rock is credited with shaping hip-hop in the 1980s.

items.title: Iran war: JD Vance heads to Pakistan for talks with Tehran
items.description: Donald Trump has tasked his vice president JD Vance with shoring up a shaky ceasefire at the high-level talks with Iran in Islamabad. Iran has threatened to break the truce over Israel's strikes on Lebanon. DW has more.

items.title: Live: Dragons fighting for their lives as Sea Eagles strike back
items.description: The NRL's only remaining winless team St George Illawarra are desperate for victory against the Sea Eagles. Later, the injury-ravaged Broncos host North Queensland. Follow live.

items.description: A man has admitted killing four young children in a kindergarten in Uganda, in an attack that has shocked the nation.

items.title: India news: Rubio to visit India for trade, Quad talks
items.description: The US Secretary of State's visit to India is likely to center around the Quad alliance and energy trade. Meanwhile, India-bound crude tankers have reportedly crossed the Strait of Hormuz. DW has more.

items.title: Democrats in US slam Pam Bondi for refusing to appear for Epstein hearing
items.description: Lawmakers warn the former Justice Department chief that a congressional subpoena for her testimony remains in effect.

items.title: Coachella Festival kicks off in California with headliners Sabrina Carpenter, Bieber and Karol G
items.description: The Coachella Festival, which kicks off in the California desert Friday, features a star-studded line-up including Justin Bieber, Karol G. and Sabrina Carpenter, and has gathered thousands of fans. French DJ and producer David Guetta returns to the desert in Indio, and David Byrne, the legendary co-founder of Talking Heads, will make an appearance.

items.title: 'Trump abandoned us': Ceasefire sparks anger, fear, division among Iranians
items.description: Iranians are divided between relief, disappointment, fear and anger after the announcement of a two-week ceasefire and possible peace plan between the USA and Israel on one side, and Iran on the other. The FRANCE 24 Observers team talked to several Iranians inside Iran to capture their responses and first-hand impressions of the ceasefire after five weeks of armed conflict in their country.

items.title: Report: Milei government not supporting campaign to restore dictatorship identities
items.description: Milei administration has interrupted sending of DNA kits to consulates nationwide for those who have doubts about their identity and suspect they may be children of disappeared. Leer más

items.description: Min Aung Hlaing seeks to 'enhance' international relations and ties with ASEAN after coup plunged Myanmar into chaos.

items.title: A fragile ceasefire and emboldened Iran
items.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with International correspondent Daniel Estrin about how the US-Iran-Israel ceasefire is being received in Israel.

items.title: Djibouti Vote: Guelleh poised to extend 27-year rule
items.description: Djibouti’s leader Ismail Omar Guelleh held his final campaign rally on Wednesday ahead of Friday’s presidential election. Facing a divided and largely silenced opposition, Guelleh is expected to extend his 27-year rule of the strategically located Horn of Africa nation.

items.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
items.description: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

items.title: Berlin opens Süd Ost Galerie, a new open-air street art landmark
items.description: Germany has unveiled a new open‑air street art destination in Berlin, where the 450‑metre Süd Ost Galerie now stretches along Grenzweg in the south‑east of the capital.

items.title: Asian stocks set for best week since 2022 ahead of US-Iran talks
#Pope Leo XIV learns to spin #basketball with Harlem Globetrotters
items.description: Members of the US exhibition basketball team the Harlem Globetrotters taught Pope Leo XIV to spin a basketball on his finger on Wednesday as they met with the pontiff at his weekly general audience in Saint Peter's Square. The moment drew loud cheers from the watching crowds.

items.title: CAF president Patrice Motsepe visits Dakar in wake of Africa Cup title drama
items.description: The president of the Confederation of African Football was in Dakar on Wednesday, just weeks after the body’s appeals board stripped Senegal of its Africa Cup title.

items.title: Iran war: Tehran claims victory but people fear what's next
items.description: As a tenuous truce takes hold after over a month of bombing, Iranians fear a wounded yet surviving regime will clamp down even more harshly on its opponents at home.

items.description: Germany's Julia Igel is to face Imane Khelif in the Algerian's first professional boxing match. Igel says she wants to use the fight to "put out a statement."

items.title: Nations tried to impose a digital fog of war in Iran. The results are mixed
items.description: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

items.title: US urges citizens to reconsider travel to Nigeria over security risks
items.description: The United States has urged its citizens to reconsider travel to Nigeria, citing risks of terrorism, civil unrest and kidnapping, according to an updated State Department advisory.

items.title: How did Pakistan bring Iran and the U.S. to the negotiating table?
items.description: Pakistan has acted as a mediator between Washington D.C. and Tehran over the last few weeks, and helped broker the ceasefire agreement this week. NPR's Juana Summers talks to Elizabeth Threlkeld, a senior fellow and director at the Stimson Center, about why Pakistan decided to step into this role.

items.title: Canada's Cohere, Germany's Aleph Alpha in merger talks, Handelsblatt reports
Melania Trump delivers statement denying Epstein ties, knowledge of his crimes
items.description: US first lady Melania Trump gave an extraordinary statement from the White House on Thursday denying any ties to late financier and convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. Trump said the "lies" linking her to Epstein were "mean-spirited attempts" to defame her reputation.

items.title: Commuters trapped for hours in freeway gridlock blast lack of signage
items.description: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

items.title: Fragile Iran truce brings brief relief to African markets
items.description: The truce between the US, Israel and Iran has eased pressure on African markets. But economists warn that deep‑rooted vulnerabilities mean the rebound may be short‑lived.

items.title: Venezuela police tear gas protesters calling for pay and pension rises
items.description: Police in Caracas on Thursday launched tear gas at thousands of protesters marching towards the presidential palace to demand pay raises and pension increases. Interim President Delcy Rodriguez announced wage increases on May 1 without disclosing the amount, as many Venezuelans live on salaries so low they struggle to survive.

items.title: Watch: Pope Leo XIV spins basketball on his finger, with help from Harlem Globetrotters
items.description: US exhibition squad, the Harlem Globetrotters, met the pontiff in the Vatican, helping him to spin a basketball on the tip of his finger.

items.title: Watch: Dramatic visuals of high-speed car chase in California
items.description: A high-speed police chase through Southern California on Wednesday (April 8) ended when sheriff’s deputies deployed a “grappler” device, sending the vehicle crashing into a home in Jurupa Valley, local media reported. The suspect was pronounced dead at the crash scene, according to ABC affiliate KABC.

items.title: Volodymyr Zelenskyy urges Russian oil sanctions to be reimposed after Iran ceasefire
items.description: The United States eased some oil sanctions on Russia last month to tackle surging energy costs caused by West Asia war

items.title: Calls to invoke the 25th Amendment after Trump's Iran threats 'useless'
items.description: US President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy “a whole civilisation” in Iran has reignited calls to invoke the 25th Amendment and drawn condemnation across the political spectrum. Yet despite a growing chorus of critics, from Democrats to former MAGA allies, the mechanism remains politically out of reach – even as Trump’s approval ratings slide ahead of the 2026 midterms.

items.title: How Trump's foreign policy is playing in North Carolina

items.title: Nigeria: Jihadists kill army general and soldiers in attack on Borno state base
items.description: The attack took place at Benisheikh, about 75 kilometres from Borno state capital Maiduguri. Military sources said unidentified jihadists overran the base and torched vehicles but soldiers repelled the raid.

items.title: Middle East live: Vance heads to Pakistan for Iran talks as Israeli assault on Lebanon tests truce
items.description: US Vice President JD Vance sets off Friday for Pakistan to lead mediated talks with Iran in a bid to find a resolution to the war as massive Israeli strikes on Lebanon this week tested a fragile US-Iran ceasefire. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu authorised direct negotiations with Lebanon that are expected in Washington next week. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

Known for shaping early hip-hop culture through DJing, electro-funk and the Zulu Nation collective, his legacy is both celebrated and contested amid longstanding abuse allegations.

items.description: The Gambia on Wednesday appointed a special prosecutor nearly two years after creating the post to try crimes committed under the reign of brutal former strongman Yahya Jammeh

items.description: Government decree lifts top officials’ wages to over eight million pesos a month, fuelling debate over austerity promises; Salary surge contrasts with Javier Milei’s pledge to cut political privilege. Leer más

items.description: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

items.title: Judge bars Trump administration from nixing protected status for Ethiopians
items.description: Ruling marks legal setback for administration's efforts to roll back protections for various groups of immigrants.

items.title: Confusion over Melania Trump's bizarrely-timed Jeffrey Epstein disavowal
items.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, April 10: Papers focus on Hungary’s opposition leader, Peter Magyar, as he confronts Prime Minister Viktor Orban in Sunday’s crucial election. Magyar is hoping to capitalise on a wave of discontent over Orban's rule. In the US, First Lady Melania Trump has convened the press at the White House to deny any ties to Jeffrey Epstein – but was it a ruse to divert attention from Donald Trump’s chaotic war in Iran? And finally, a new study sheds light on a community of chimpanzees that waged a bloody civil war among themselves.

items.title: Pakistan’s return: Mediator or West Asia asset?
items.description: Across India and among South Asia watchers, Pakistan’s role has been met with a mix of surprise and unease, with sentiments that are increasingly visible in sections of strategic commentary

items.title: Brazil: Indigenous marches in Brasília enter third day over land rights
items.description: Thousands of Indigenous people marched through Brasília on 9 April 2026 during the 22nd “Free Land Encampment”, urging authorities to recognise ancestral lands ahead of October’s elections.

items.title: Almodóvar, Travolta and Bardem lead 2026 Cannes lineup
items.description: Film critic Ben Croll joins Eve Jackson to break down this year’s Cannes lineup – from the biggest names to the early buzz, and what it reveals about the state of global cinema. The 2026 Cannes Film Festival is already shaping up to be one of the most talked-about editions in years. Major directors including Pedro Almodóvar, Steven Soderbergh and Ron Howard are set to premiere new work on the Croisette. They are joined by a host of international stars – among them Javier Bardem, Michael Fassbender and Kristen Stewart – underlining Cannes’ enduring global appeal.

items.title: Elon Musk's xAI sues Colorado over state's new AI law

items.title: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel tells NBC News that he will not step down
items.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has told NBC News' Meet the Press that he would not step down in his first interview with a U.S. network.

items.title: Life a 'continuous nightmare' for family of woman set alight by her son
items.description: NSW grandmother Nerol Doble was murdered by her son Shannon James Doble in 2024 after an argument over the use of a jerry can. During a sentencing hearing, family members told the court of the hell his actions have left them in.

items.title: World Bank sees Argentina’s economy growing 3.6% this year
items.description: Economy will grow 3.6% this year and 3.7% next year; Multilateral institution’s forecast means Argentina is currently on track to record three years of consecutive growth for first time since 2008. Leer más

items.description: As UK PM Keir Starmer arrived in Doha as part of a Gulf tour he discussed efforts to secure the US-Iran ceasefire.

items.title: US: Pentagon must restore journalists' access, judge says
items.description: A federal judge has said the US Defense Department has not complied with orders to allow journalists access to the Pentagon. New rules have prevented reporters from entering the building without an escort.

items.title: Authorities in Argentina say fatal school shooting linked online ‘TCC’ crime community
items.description: Security Minister Alejandra Monteoliva says San Cristóbal school shooting was not “an isolated case nor a case of bullying,” claiming the shooter “participated in an international digital network” and Internet subculture dedicated to such incidents. Leer más

items.description: Emergency workers in Beirut are searching for survivors after Israeli strikes on busy commercial and residential areas of the Lebanese capital on Wednesday. Now there are fears the Middle East ceasefire is at risk.

items.title: Milei’s controversial glacier reform bill approved by Congress

items.description: Authorities in Djibouti dispatch ballot papers and boxes on the eve of the country's presidential election. Incumbent Ismail Omar Guelleh, in power since 1999, is expected to win the election, facing a divided and largely silenced opposition.

items.title: Australia and Singapore aim to meet fuel supply needs as crisis continues
items.description: Australia and Singapore have agreed to make "maximum efforts" to meet each other's fuel and gas needs as the crisis in the Middle East continues, with the prime minister securing an encouraging but not legally binding deal.

items.description: After 14 days trapped in a flooded gold and silver mine, a worker has been pulled out alive while two others have died.

items.description: United Nations Secretary-General spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric condemned a drone strike in Sudan on Thursday that killed 30 people at a wedding in North Darfur state.

items.description: US House of Representatives Republicans blocked a Democratic effort to end US attacks on Iran, as Donald Trump’s party continued to prevent efforts to rein in the Republican president’s war powers.

items.description: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

items.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country

items.title: Somalia, the Islamic State group's new front line in Africa

Local authorities have launched a major offensive, driving fighters from villages into cave positions amid ongoing clashes and mounting casualties, in a campaign officials say is crucial to restoring stability in Somalia and countering a broader global security threat. A report by Bastien Renouil, Will Swanson and ARTE.

items.title: Who profits from the Iran war? Strait of Hormuz toll talk fuels outrage
items.description: A most confusing Wednesday it was. In the space of six minutes, during a single press conference, the White House spokesperson first hailed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, then called on Iran to reopen the world’s most oil-sensitive shipping lane. Five weeks of contradictory statements have bred wider uncertainty over the true contents of the ceasefire deal – and even who is party to it. Confusion moves markets, and in a war that has triggered the most severe energy crisis in decades, oil prices reflect that volatility: having fallen sharply on Wednesday, they have since climbed back above $100 a barrel.

items.description: The fragile truce between Iran and the United States showed signs of unravelling on Thursday, with Tehran threatening to resume hostilities as Israel pummelled Lebanon.

items.title: CAF President Motsepe rejects Senegal’s corruption claim amid AFCON fallout
items.description: Senegal's government alleged corruption at African football's governing body after AFCON title awarded to Morocco.

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: News24 | JD Vance slams Zelensky’s ‘completely scandalous’ comments on Hungary’s Orban
items.description: US Vice President JD ‌Vance said that Ukraine’s president had made “scandalous” comments about Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban, as he echoed Budapest’s accusations that Kyiv was using energy supplies to try to influence elections there.

items.title: Live: Magpies locked in tense encounter with Dockers at Gather Round
items.description: Collingwood welcomes back two of its key players, as it takes on fourth-placed Fremantle at Adelaide Oval. Follow live.

items.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
items.description: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

items.title: Donald Trump's tenuous Iran exit plan isn't healing Republican rifts exposed by the war
items.description: Former Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene of Georgia, a Mr. Trump supporter-turned-critic, called for the President to be removed from office through the Constitution's 25th Amendment

items.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
items.description: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

items.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
items.description: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

items.title: Russia labels human rights group Memorial 'extremist'
items.description: Russia's Supreme Court deemed the storied rights organization Memorial "clearly anti-Russian" in a closed-door ruling. Memorial said the decision marked a new phase of repression in Russia.

items.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
items.description: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

items.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
items.description: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

items.title: News24 | Trump pushes for Strait of Hormuz reopening without tolls, White House says
items.description: ‌US President Donald Trump wants to ⁠see the Strait of Hormuz ⁠open up for oil tankers and other traffic without any limitations, including tolls, White House spokeswoman Karoline ‌Leavitt said on Wednesday.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to truce for Orthodox Easter
items.description: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon on 11 April through Easter Sunday.

items.title: News24 | China tankers join line to test Hormuz exit and Iran truce
items.description: Two fully laden Chinese oil tankers are waiting outside the Strait of Hormuz, putting them in a position to become the first such vessels to leave the Persian Gulf under a day-old US-Iran ceasefire, even as shipowners scrutinise the status of the narrow waterway.

items.title: Benin faces uneven race for president
items.description: Backed by the ruling bloc, Romuald Wadagni, 49, who has been finance minister for 10 years, faces a single rival, Paul Hounkpe, a former culture minister seen as a moderate

items.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough on Ukraine, poll shows
items.description: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

items.title: News24 | US-Iran ceasefire: Temporary truce amid ongoing tensions as Trump agrees to 2-week pause
items.description: US President Donald Trump declared victory after Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire on Tuesday, ending a conflict that began on 28 February when the US and Israel launched co-ordinated military strikes against the Islamic Republic.

items.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend
items.description: An old favourite, new Courtney Barnett and a tasty Korean vegetarian stew.

items.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to 32-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
items.description: Moscow and Kyiv signal a short Easter truce as diplomacy stalls and war pressures mount.

items.title: The YPF case and Argentina’s New York battle scars
items.description: The Argentine Republic is a veteran of many battles in New York Courthouses and the YPF case is one of its most epic stand-offs. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Former US attorney general Pam Bondi plans to skip Congress appearance on Epstein files
items.description: Former US Attorney General Pam Bondi does not plan to appear for a planned interview with a House of Representatives ‌committee on the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files following her firing by US President Donald Trump, the Justice Department told Congress.

items.title: India proposes new rules to regulate news and political posts on social media
items.description: The changes will give government more power over news-related posts shared by podcasters and influencers.

items.title: 68 gender-based killings in Argentina in first quarter, report finds
items.description: Report by La Casa del Encuentro NGO recorded 68 gender-based killings in January-March period; 72% of killings took place in victim’s home. Leer más

items.title: Trump criticizes NATO over Iran in meeting with Rutte
items.description: President Donald Trump said NATO "wasn't there" after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran. But NATO head Mark Rutte said "a large majority of European countries" lived up to their side of the deal.

items.title: News24 | Furious Trump slams former MAGA commentators as ‘nut jobs’ over Iran war criticism
items.description: US President Donald Trump angrily lashed out at multiple well-known conservative commentators who have criticised his war against Iran, slamming his onetime allies as attention-seeking “NUT JOBS”.

items.title: Myanmar junta chief sworn in as President
items.description: Democracy watchdogs deride the transition as a rebranding of military rule in an unconvincing civilian disguise

items.title: Argentina’s inequality eases slightly, but income gap remains stark
items.description: Income inequality improved slightly towards end of 2025; INDEC data shows richest 10% still earn 13 times more than poorest, with sharp disparities in gender and informal employment persisting. Leer más

items.title: News24 | Trump weighs pulling some US troops from Europe amid NATO strains, official says
items.description: US President Donald Trump, upset at NATO allies’ failure to ⁠help secure the Strait of Hormuz and angry that his plans to acquire Greenland have ⁠not moved forward, has discussed with advisersthe option of removing some US troops from Europe, a senior White House official told Reuters on Thursday.

items.title: Paul Hounkpè, the moderate challenger facing favourite Wadagni in Benin vote
items.description: Paul Hounkpè, of the Cowry Forces for ⁠an Emerging Benin party, is the only opposition candidate in Benin's presidential election.

items.title: Israeli strikes on Beirut kill at least 182 and wound hundreds
items.description: Lebanon spent the night digging through rubble after Israeli strikes hit busy commercial and residential districts in central Beirut late on Wednesday, killing at least 182 people and wounding 890, according to the health ministry.

items.title: News24 | Automatic US draft pool registration for 18-year-olds part of Trump ‘options’ for war
items.description: Eligible US men aged 18 to 25 will now be automatically registered for the military draft pool by December as part of an effort to streamline the previous process of self-registration and save money.

items.title: Questions mount for Milei's Cabinet Chief Manuel Adorni as probes examine assets, travel
items.description: President Javier Milei’s Cabinet chief is facing mounting questions over his finances after journalistic investigations uncovered inconsistencies, unexplained asset growth and transactions involving third parties. Leer más

items.title: Expelled Iran envoy says decision ‘does not benefit Argentina’
items.description: Iran’s chargé d’affaires Mohsen Soltani Tehrani criticises decision by President Javier MIlei’s government to expel him from Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
items.description: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

items.title: Infant found dead inside vehicle in Palmerston driveway
items.description: Northern Territory Police are investigating the death of an infant who was found inside a vehicle parked in the driveway of a Palmerston home on Friday.

items.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
items.description: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

items.title: Deported to Eswatini: Cambodian refugee questions controversial US policy
items.description: Pheap Rom spent decades building a life in the United States, holding permanent resident status since 1985. But today, he finds himself detained in Eswatini, far from the country he once called home.

items.title: Extreme heat and weather conditions attributed to stagnant jet stream
items.description: It’s no coincidence that extreme heat is engulfing huge swaths of Asia, Europe and North America all at the same time.

items.title: China's factories snap years-long deflation spell on Iran war price shock
War in Iran strands tons of tea in Kenyan port
items.description: Iran war-linked shipping disruptions have stranded 8 million kg of tea in the Kenyan port of Mombasa, costing the industry about $8 million a week in mounting losses.

items.title: Anthropic weighs building its own AI chips, sources say
Ukraine: Russia's Putin declares Easter ceasefire
items.description: Russian President Putin has announced a ceasefire in its war in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter holiday. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said his country will "act accordingly" during the truce.

items.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
items.description: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

items.title: Israeli military says it has killed nephew of Hezbollah chief Naim Qassem
items.description: Israel PM Benjamin Netanyahu said that Israel would keep hitting Iran-backed Hezbollah “wherever necessary”, the day after Israeli strikes pummelled Lebanon

items.title: News24 | Zimbabwe aims to push through constitutional amendment for Mnangagwa to serve until 2030
items.description: A proposed Zimbabwean constitutional amendment will extend the term of the current president, Emmerson Mnangagwa, to 2030.

items.title: Emperor penguins listed as endangered species: IUCN
items.description: The largest and heaviest of their species, boasting a brilliant golden-orange streak on the neck and chest, emperor penguins have become symbolic of the quest to thrive and survive in Antarctica’s harsh climes

items.title: Gladio: NATO’s secret cold war operation in Italy
items.description: One of Europe’s biggest state secrets – and a scandal that still shakes Italy today – was a clandestine army set up by NATO after World War II to block the spread of communism. During the Cold War, cells of the network operated in around ten European countries. In Italy, the covert operation was known by the code name Gladio. Natalia Mendoza, Tommaso Marro, and Laura Roudaut report.

items.title: Watch: BBC asks Artemis II crew a question in space
items.description: The BBC’s Science Editor Rebecca Morelle has spoken to the Artemis II crew ahead of their likely splashdown on Saturday.

items.title: Israel’s Lebanon strikes kill 203, sparking global calls to extend U.S.-Iran truce
items.description: There had been conflicting diplomatic signals about whether the fighting in Lebanon was included in the U.S.-Iran truce — but Washington said that it was not and Israel made it clear that it has no intention of holding off

items.title: News24 | Dutch minister says not planning to bar Kanye West
items.description: The Dutch minister says there are no plans to ban Kanye West (Ye) from the Netherlands, despite the UK banning him.
